name: Bible Wisdom
description: Timeless verses of faith, hope, and love from the pages of Scripture.
author: Quips Editorial

quotes.content: Trust in the LORD with all thine heart; and lean not unto thine own understanding.
quotes.notes: A cornerstone proverb on faith over self-reliance, paired with verse 6's promise of directed paths.

quotes.content: In all thy ways acknowledge him, and he shall direct thy paths.
quotes.notes: The completion of the trust exhortation begun in Proverbs 3:5.

quotes.content: The LORD is my shepherd; I shall not want.
quotes.notes: The opening line of the beloved shepherd psalm attributed to David.

quotes.content: Yea, though I walk through the valley of the shadow of death, I will fear no evil: for thou art with me; thy rod and thy staff they comfort me.
quotes.notes: The psalm's assurance of God's presence amid mortal danger.

quotes.content: God is our refuge and strength, a very present help in trouble.
quotes.notes: The opening declaration of a psalm celebrating divine steadfastness.

quotes.content: Be still, and know that I am God.
quotes.notes: A call to stillness and trust amid turmoil, one of Scripture's most quoted lines.

quotes.content: I will lift up mine eyes unto the hills, from whence cometh my help.
quotes.notes: The opening of a psalm of ascents about God as keeper and protector.

quotes.content: This is the day which the LORD hath made; we will rejoice and be glad in it.
quotes.notes: A verse of daily thanksgiving common in worship and prayer.

quotes.content: Thy word is a lamp unto my feet, and a light unto my path.
quotes.notes: From the longest psalm, a meditation on the guidance of Scripture.

quotes.content: To every thing there is a season, and a time to every purpose under the heaven.
quotes.notes: The famous opening of the Preacher's poem on the seasons of life.

quotes.content: A time to weep, and a time to laugh; a time to mourn, and a time to dance.
quotes.notes: Part of Ecclesiastes' catalog of life's appointed times.

quotes.content: Blessed are the poor in spirit: for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.
quotes.notes: The first of the Beatitudes in Jesus' Sermon on the Mount.

quotes.content: Blessed are the meek: for they shall inherit the earth.
quotes.notes: A Beatitude promising reward to the humble.

quotes.content: Blessed are the peacemakers: for they shall be called the children of God.
quotes.notes: A Beatitude honoring those who make peace.

quotes.content: Ye are the light of the world. A city that is set on an hill cannot be hid.
quotes.notes: Jesus' call for his followers to be visible witnesses.

quotes.content: For God so loved the world, that he gave his only begotten Son, that whosoever believeth in him should not perish, but have everlasting life.
quotes.notes: Perhaps the most widely quoted verse in the New Testament.

quotes.content: And ye shall know the truth, and the truth shall make you free.
quotes.notes: Jesus' words to believers on the liberating power of truth.

quotes.content: And we know that all things work together for good to them that love God, to them who are the called according to his purpose.
quotes.notes: Paul's assurance of God's providence over all circumstances.

quotes.content: Charity suffereth long, and is kind; charity envieth not; charity vaunteth not itself, is not puffed up.
quotes.notes: From Paul's hymn to love; the KJV renders agape as 'charity.'

quotes.content: And now abideth faith, hope, charity, these three; but the greatest of these is charity.
quotes.notes: The closing line of the love chapter, exalting charity above all.

quotes.content: I can do all things through Christ which strengtheneth me.
quotes.notes: Paul's testimony of contentment and strength written from prison.

quotes.content: But they that wait upon the LORD shall renew their strength; they shall mount up with wings as eagles; they shall run, and not be weary; and they shall walk, and not faint.
quotes.notes: Isaiah's promise of renewal to those who hope in God.

quotes.content: He hath shewed thee, O man, what is good; and what doth the LORD require of thee, but to do justly, and to love mercy, and to walk humbly with thy God?
quotes.notes: The prophet Micah's summary of true religion.

quotes.content: For I know the thoughts that I think toward you, saith the LORD, thoughts of peace, and not of evil, to give you an expected end.
quotes.notes: God's message of hope to exiles through the prophet Jeremiah.

quotes.content: Be strong and of a good courage; be not afraid, neither be thou dismayed: for the LORD thy God is with thee whithersoever thou goest.
quotes.notes: God's commission to Joshua as he leads Israel into the promised land.

quotes.content: A soft answer turneth away wrath: but grievous words stir up anger.
quotes.notes: A proverb on the power of gentle speech.

quotes.content: Train up a child in the way he should go: and when he is old, he will not depart from it.
quotes.notes: A well-known proverb on the lasting influence of upbringing.

quotes.content: Iron sharpeneth iron; so a man sharpeneth the countenance of his friend.
quotes.notes: A proverb celebrating the refining influence of friendship.

quotes.content: Weeping may endure for a night, but joy cometh in the morning.
quotes.notes: A psalm of David on the fleeting nature of sorrow.

quotes.content: The LORD bless thee, and keep thee: The LORD make his face shine upon thee, and be gracious unto thee.
quotes.notes: The opening of the Aaronic priestly blessing.

quotes.content: Rejoice evermore. Pray without ceasing. In every thing give thanks.
quotes.notes: Paul's threefold exhortation to the Thessalonian church.
